prompt: Piaget's sensorimotor stage (birth to ~2) is characterized by:

options.text: Learning through senses and motor actions; object permanence develops
Abstract hypothetical reasoning exclusively
Formal operational thought about algebra always
Industry vs inferiority psychosocial crisis primarily

explanation: Infants explore world physically; object permanence emerges late in stage.

distractorExplanations.d: Erikson industry stage is school age.

prompt: A student with autism may benefit from:

options.text: Constant unpredictable changes to reduce anxiety always
Visual schedules, predictable routines, and explicit social skills instruction
Withholding all visual supports always
Only group work with no individual accommodations ever

explanation: Structure and visual supports reduce anxiety and clarify expectations.

prompt: Developmental delay means a child performs substantially below age expectations in:

options.text: Only athletic ability exclusively always for legal eligibility always
Adult employment history always
One or more developmental domains
College coursework exclusively always

explanation: Delay affects domains such as cognitive, communication, motor, social, adaptive.

prompt: Theory of Mind difficulties may appear in students who:

options.text: Always exceed peers in social cognition automatically always
Never have communication challenges ever
Only struggle with physical education exclusively always
Struggle to understand others' perspectives and beliefs

explanation: Theory of mind involves inferring others' mental states; common in ASD.

prompt: Adolescents with intellectual disability still need instruction in:

options.text: Transition planning for post-school living, work, and community participation
Only preschool-level colors with no functional skills ever always
Exclusively abstract calculus with no life skills ever always
Nothing after age 14 by federal law always

explanation: IDEA requires transition services by age 16 (some states earlier).

prompt: Early intervention services under Part C serve:

options.text: Only adults in vocational rehab exclusively always
Infants and toddlers with disabilities or developmental delays
College students exclusively always
Only gifted students without disabilities always

explanation: Part C (IDEA) supports birth through age 2 (state variation in extend to 3).

prompt: Executive function skills include:

options.text: Only rote spelling of random words exclusively always
Maximum running speed exclusively always
Working memory, inhibitory control, and cognitive flexibility
Memorizing state capitals only without application ever always

explanation: Executive functions manage goal-directed behavior and self-regulation.

prompt: Attachment theory suggests consistent responsive caregiving:

options.text: Has no effect on child development ever according to research always
Should be eliminated in all schools always
Only matters after age 18 exclusively always
Supports secure attachment and emotional development

explanation: Secure attachment correlates with emotional regulation and exploration.

prompt: A student with ADHD may demonstrate:

options.text: Inattention, hyperactivity, and/or impulsivity affecting functioning
Guaranteed superior focus in all settings always
No need for organizational supports ever always
Only physical disability with no cognitive impact ever always

explanation: ADHD symptoms must impair functioning in multiple settings for diagnosis.

prompt: Language development milestones: by 12 months many children:

options.text: Read chapter books fluently always
Use one or few words and understand simple requests
Write five-paragraph essays always
Have adult conversational grammar fully always

explanation: First words typically emerge around first birthday (wide variation).

prompt: Sensory processing differences may lead a student to:

options.text: Never react to any sensory input ever always
Only affect math computation exclusively always
Seek or avoid certain sounds, textures, or lights
Eliminate need for any IEP services always automatically

explanation: Sensory over- or under-responsivity affects participation and regulation.

prompt: Zone of Proximal Development (Vygotsky) refers to:

options.text: Tasks always impossible even with help always forever
Only tasks mastered years ago always exclusively
Adult workplace tasks exclusively in kindergarten always
Tasks a learner can do with guidance but not yet independently

explanation: ZPD is space between independent and assisted performance.

prompt: Down syndrome is caused by:

options.text: Trisomy 21 (extra copy of chromosome 21)
Bacterial infection during school hours exclusively always
Poor teaching exclusively as sole cause always
Lack of homework exclusively always genetically

explanation: Most cases involve three copies of chromosome 21.

prompt: Prerequisite skills for reading include:

options.text: Only calculus readiness exclusively always
Phonological awareness, letter knowledge, and vocabulary
Advanced driving skills exclusively always
No oral language exposure ever beneficial always

explanation: Early literacy builds on sound awareness, print concepts, language.

prompt: Trauma-informed practice in schools emphasizes:

options.text: Punishing all behavioral reactions without context always
Ignoring student history entirely always
Safety, trust, choice, collaboration, and empowerment
Removing all routines to increase chaos always

explanation: Trauma-informed care prioritizes physical/emotional safety and relationships.

prompt: An IEP must include:

options.text: Only the student's favorite color always
Teacher salary information exclusively always
Unlimited promises without services defined ever always
Present levels, measurable annual goals, services, and placement

explanation: IDEA requires specific IEP components documented annually.

prompt: Least Restrictive Environment (LRE) means:

options.text: Educating students with disabilities alongside nondisabled peers to maximum appropriate extent
Always placing every student in separate school always without consideration ever
Eliminating all specialized instruction always instantly always
Only educating students at home always without team decision ever always

explanation: LRE presumes inclusion with supplementary aids/services as needed.

prompt: Accommodations differ from modifications because accommodations:

options.text: Always lower grade-level expectations always by definition always
Change how a student accesses content without altering grade-level standards
Eliminate all assessment ever always
Are only for general education students never special education ever always

explanation: Accommodations adjust access; modifications change what is taught/expected.

prompt: A BIP (Behavior Intervention Plan) should be based on:

options.text: Random penalties without data always
Only academic grades in unrelated subjects always exclusively
Functional Behavioral Assessment identifying function of behavior
Parent preference alone without assessment ever always sufficient always

explanation: FBA informs function-based positive interventions in BIP.

prompt: Progress monitoring for IEP goals should be:

options.text: Collected only once at graduation exclusively always
Based solely on teacher intuition without records ever always
Hidden from parents always by law always
Frequent, standardized or curriculum-based, and used to adjust instruction

explanation: Regular progress data informs whether goals are met and services effective.

prompt: Co-teaching models may include:

options.text: One teach/one assist, station teaching, parallel teaching, alternative teaching, team teaching
Only one teacher leaving the room always exclusively always
Special educator never collaborating with general educator always always
Students teaching exclusively with no adult ever always in all sped classes always

explanation: Friend & Cook co-teaching approaches vary by lesson purpose.

prompt: Extended School Year (ESY) services are provided when:

options.text: Any parent requests summer camp always automatically qualifies always
Regression/recoupment data show significant skill loss over breaks
Student is gifted without disability always qualifies always
Never documented in IDEA ever always

explanation: ESY eligibility based on regression, recoupment, critical skills, etc.

prompt: Universal Design for Learning (UDL) promotes:

options.text: One fixed worksheet for all learners always exclusively always
Eliminating all technology always as UDL principle always
Multiple means of engagement, representation, and action/expression
Teaching only through lecture without variation ever always

explanation: UDL reduces barriers proactively through flexible options.

prompt: Related services on an IEP may include:

options.text: Only varsity athletics exclusively always as related service always automatically
Luxury vacations for families always funded by district always
Unrelated cosmetic services always
Speech-language therapy, occupational therapy, and transportation if required

explanation: IDEA lists related services that assist benefit from special education.

prompt: Prior Written Notice must be given when:

options.text: District proposes or refuses to change identification, evaluation, or placement
Student completes homework on time always
Cafeteria menu changes always
Daily attendance is taken always without any decision ever always

explanation: PWN documents proposed/refused actions and parental rights.

prompt: SMART IEP goals are:

options.text: Secret, Mandatory, Arbitrary, Random, Temporary always as acronym always officially
Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound
Only social goals never academic ever allowed always
Optional under IDEA always without measure ever always

explanation: Measurable goals with clear criteria and timelines are required.

prompt: Differentiated instruction for diverse learners includes:

options.text: Identical task for everyone regardless of readiness always always
Tracking solely by age with no data ever always
Adjusting content, process, product, or environment based on student need
Eliminating all scaffolding always as best practice always always

explanation: Tomlinson's differentiation responds to readiness, interest, profile.

prompt: Manifestation determination reviews occur when:

options.text: Student earns honor roll always
Parent attends conference always without discipline ever always
Never required under IDEA ever always
Disciplinary removal exceeds 10 consecutive days or constitutes pattern

explanation: Team determines if conduct caused by or related to disability.

prompt: Transition services in the IEP focus on:

options.text: Postsecondary education, employment, and independent living skills
Only elementary recess activities exclusively always
Retirement planning at age 10 exclusively always
Eliminating all community-based instruction always always

explanation: Transition planning prepares for life after secondary school.

prompt: Assistive technology on an IEP:

options.text: Is prohibited under IDEA always
Must be considered for every student with a disability
Replaces special education entirely always automatically always
Only includes motorized vehicles always exclusively always

explanation: IDEA requires AT consideration; range from low to high tech.

prompt: Norm-referenced tests compare a student's performance to:

options.text: Only the student's prior day mood exclusively always
An arbitrary score with no reference group ever always
A representative sample of peers (norm group)
Teacher's personal opinion exclusively always without data ever always

explanation: Norm-referenced scores (percentiles, standard scores) reference peer distribution.

prompt: Criterion-referenced assessment measures:

options.text: Only relative standing among national peers exclusively always without skill definition ever always
Stock market indices exclusively always
Random guessing exclusively always as design always
Performance against a defined skill standard or criterion

explanation: Criterion-referenced shows mastery of specific objectives.

prompt: A standard score of 100 on an IQ test typically represents:

options.text: The mean of the norm sample
Maximum possible gifted score always
Failure always
Grade equivalent of kindergarten always exclusively always

explanation: IQ tests standardize mean=100, SD=15 (most tests).

prompt: Curriculum-Based Measurement (CBM) is useful because it:

options.text: Requires full-day testing weekly always exclusively always
Provides frequent, brief probes to track academic progress
Cannot be graphed over time ever always
Only measures behavior never academics ever always

explanation: CBM (e.g., oral reading fluency) enables progress monitoring graphs.

prompt: Assessment bias should be minimized by:

options.text: Ignoring student language background always always
Testing only in non-native language always without support ever always as policy always
Using culturally fair procedures and interpreting scores cautiously
Assuming all tests are perfectly fair without review ever always

explanation: Evaluators consider language, culture, disability in interpretation.

prompt: A discrepancy model for SLD identification compares:

options.text: Shoe size to height exclusively always as federal requirement always
Only attendance records exclusively always
Parent income exclusively always as sole criterion always federally always
Achievement to cognitive ability (historically; many states shifting)

explanation: IQ-achievement discrepancy was common; RTI now emphasized in many states.

prompt: Response to Intervention (RTI) uses:

options.text: Tiered increasingly intensive instruction with progress monitoring
Only retention without intervention always always
Single test on one day to label student permanently always always
Elimination of all general education support always always

explanation: RTI provides data-based tiers before/during special education evaluation.

prompt: Dynamic assessment examines:

options.text: Only memorized facts without teaching during test ever always exclusively always
Learning potential during mediated teaching rather than static performance only
Physical fitness exclusively always
Parent survey exclusively always without student interaction ever always exclusively always

explanation: Dynamic testing measures responsiveness to instruction during assessment.

prompt: Ecological assessment gathers information from:

options.text: Only standardized clinic with no context ever always exclusively always
A single multiple-choice item always sufficient always
Multiple environments such as classroom, home, and community
Historical documents from 1800 exclusively always only always

explanation: Ecological approach observes behavior across settings.

prompt: Portfolio assessment includes:

options.text: Only one test score from one day always exclusively always
Random deletion of all student work always as practice always
Exclusive use for college admissions always in lieu of IEP always always federally always
Collected work samples showing growth over time

explanation: Portfolios document performance and progress authentically.

prompt: Inter-rater reliability refers to:

options.text: Consistency of scoring across different evaluators
Student mood on one day exclusively always
Test length in pages exclusively always
Cost of testing materials exclusively always

explanation: High inter-rater reliability means scorers agree on observations/scores.

prompt: A functional behavioral assessment collects data on:

options.text: Only student eye color exclusively always
Antecedents, behavior, and consequences (ABC)
District budget exclusively always
Unrelated standardized math norms exclusively always without behavior observation ever always

explanation: ABC analysis identifies triggers and maintaining consequences.

prompt: Testing accommodations on state assessments may include:

options.text: Changing scored content always illegally always
Providing answers to student always as accommodation always always
Extended time, separate setting, or read-aloud per IEP/504
Eliminating participation always for all disabled students always always federally always

explanation: Accommodations change access not what is measured (unless alternate assessment).

prompt: Age equivalent scores are often discouraged because:

options.text: They are always the most precise metric always preferred always always
They replace standard scores legally always always
They measure only behavior never achievement ever always
They imply uniform skills at an age and can mislead about scatter

explanation: Age equivalents oversimplify profile and vary by subtest scatter.

prompt: Parent consent is required before:

options.text: Initial special education evaluation
Every daily lunch choice always
Routine fire drill always as evaluation always
Homework assignment in general education always as sped evaluation always always

explanation: IDEA requires informed consent for initial evaluation.

prompt: IDEA stands for:

options.text: International Development Education Association exclusively always
Individuals with Disabilities Education Act
Integrated Daily Exercise Activity exclusively always federally always
Internal District Employment Agreement exclusively always always

explanation: IDEA is federal special education law (formerly EHA).

prompt: FAPE under IDEA means:

options.text: Free Athletic Programs exclusively for all students always always
Fully Advanced Private Education paid for all always without limits always
Free Appropriate Public Education tailored to student needs
Optional services only if district chooses always without requirement ever always

explanation: FAPE requires special education and related services at no cost to parents.

prompt: Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act prohibits:

options.text: All homework assignments nationally always
Use of any accommodations ever always
Parent involvement always exclusively always
Disability discrimination in programs receiving federal funds

explanation: 504 provides accommodations for qualifying disabilities; differs from IDEA.

prompt: Child Find requires schools to:

options.text: Identify, locate, and evaluate children who may need special education
Wait until parents request services at age 21 only always exclusively always
Exclude private school children entirely always without exception ever always
Only serve students with physical disabilities always always exclusively always

explanation: Child Find is ongoing obligation to find eligible children.

distractorExplanations.c: Some services for parentally placed private.

prompt: Zero reject principle means:

options.text: Students may be permanently expelled without education always always
No eligible child with a disability is denied access to public education
Only top academic performers receive services always always
Districts may refuse evaluation always without reason ever always

explanation: IDEA requires education for all eligible children regardless of severity.

prompt: Procedural safeguards notice must inform parents of:

options.text: Cafeteria prices exclusively always only always
Teacher dress code exclusively always only always
Rights to consent, due process, and dispute resolution
Athletic team rankings exclusively always only always

explanation: Parents receive rights information annually and at key points.

prompt: Due process hearing may be requested when:

options.text: Student prefers one lunch item always
Weather cancels recess always
Teacher assigns seating alphabetically always without dispute always always
Parents and district disagree on identification, evaluation, placement, or FAPE

explanation: Impartial hearing resolves IDEA disputes.

prompt: NCLB/ESEA reauthorizations influenced special education by:

options.text: Emphasizing accountability and participation of students with disabilities in assessments
Eliminating all testing for students with IEPs always permanently always
Removing LRE requirement always always
Transferring IDEA to local parks departments always always

explanation: Accountability laws address inclusion in state testing with accommodations.

prompt: Brown v. Board of Education (1954) is foundational because it:

options.text: Established IDEA exclusively in 1954 always historically inaccurate always
Declared separate educational facilities inherently unequal
Eliminated all special education always always
Mandated standardized testing exclusively always in 1954 always always

explanation: Brown ended legal segregation; influenced inclusion movements.

prompt: PL 94-142 (1975) established:

options.text: Mandatory military service in high school always always
Elimination of parent participation always always
Right to education for children with disabilities (precursor to IDEA)
Private-only education funding always always

explanation: EHA/PL 94-142 guaranteed FAPE in LRE.

prompt: Confidentiality of student records under FERPA means:

options.text: Records posted publicly always without consent always
Teachers may share IEP with anyone always without permission always
Parents may never access records always always
Personally identifiable information is protected with limited disclosure

explanation: FERPA protects education records; parents have access rights.

prompt: Professional ethical practice for special educators includes:

options.text: Maintaining competence, confidentiality, and advocating for students
Disclosing student disabilities publicly to all classmates always always
Ignoring colleagues in collaboration always always
Using restraint without training always as first option always always

explanation: CEC ethical codes emphasize dignity, collaboration, evidence-based practice.

distractorExplanations.d: Restraint only when trained and necessary.

prompt: Inclusive education research generally shows:

options.text: Segregation always produces superior outcomes always for everyone always without exception always
Students with disabilities benefit academically and socially with supports
Peers never benefit from inclusion always always research shows opposite trends often always
Inclusion eliminates need for specialized instruction always always instantly always

explanation: Inclusion with supports benefits many; specialized instruction continues.

distractorExplanations.a: LRE research supports inclusion when appropriate.

prompt: Cultural and linguistic diversity in evaluation requires:

options.text: Assuming low scores always reflect disability without considering language always always acceptable always
Ignoring interpreter use always always
Assessments in native language when feasible and nondiscriminatory tools
Using only one monolingual test always without documentation ever always always

explanation: Evaluators distinguish language difference from disability.

prompt: Mediation in special education disputes is:

options.text: Mandatory binding arbitration always without parent consent always always federally always required always
Unavailable under IDEA always always
Only for general education grading disputes always always exclusively always
Voluntary facilitated negotiation before or instead of due process

explanation: IDEA offers mediation as confidential dispute resolution option.
